g.text: prefix to chat with the LLM.
> What capabilities do you have for working with git repositories? Answer in 2 sentences.
The user is asking about my git repository capabilities. Let me check what the git function can do based on its
- Execute Git operations on a repository
- Supported operations depend on configuration. Default allows:
- diff: Show changes (can target specific file with path parameter)
- show: Show various types of objects
- Destructive operations (commit, push) require explicit permission
So I can read git information (status, logs, diffs, branches, show objects) by default, but destructive operations
like committing or pushing would need special permission.
I should answer in 2 sentences as requested.
I can execute read-only Git operations including viewing repository status, commit logs, diffs (for entire repos or
specific files), branch lists, and showing various Git objects. Destructive operations like committing or pushing
require explicit permission and are not enabled by default.
> Use the git tool to show the current repository status. Reply in 1 line.
The user wants me to use the git tool to show the current repository status. I should use the git function with the
The git status shows the repository is on branch "feature/14-skill-infrastructure" and is up to date with origin.
There are 3 untracked files but nothing staged for commit. I need to reply in 1 line.
On branch feature/14-skill-infrastructure, up to date with origin, with 3 untracked files (demos/README.md,
demos/skills.md, media/demo-skills.svg) and nothing to commit.
